title: aria graeca
intro: Aria graeca, also known as Greek valerian, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small white flowers and lance-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a sedative and anxiolytic due to the presence of valepotriates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aria graeca include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and relief from muscle and joint pain. Aria graeca can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and calmness.
